svg.title: Modular 16S Satellite BMS ASIC Block Diagram
svg.desc: Architecture block diagram showing signal flow from 16-cell battery stack through AFE-I, DFP, HAE-SOC, A-BAL, D-COMM, and P-MGMT blocks with labeled nets.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the 16S series battery stack and how sense wires connect to the AFE-I')
sendPrompt('What does the AFE-I analog front-end do in a BMS ASIC and why is it placed near the top pins?')
g.text: ◀ The Eyes — digitizes all cell data ▶

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the DFP Digital Fault Processor block in a BMS ASIC — what faults does it detect and why is the Trip path zero-latency?')
sendPrompt('Explain the HAE-SOC hardware accelerated estimator — how does it compute State of Charge and State of Health?')
sendPrompt('Explain the A-BAL active balancer block — passive vs active balancing and how it uses SoX data from HAE-SOC?')
sendPrompt('Why does the Trip net go directly to the external fault pad without passing through any other logic block?')
sendPrompt('What happens at the external fault pad when a cell overvoltage is detected?')
sendPrompt('Explain the D-COMM communication hub in BMS ASIC — what protocols does it use (CAN, SMBus, I2C) and why is it placed far from the AFE-I?')
sendPrompt('What vehicle-level signals does the BMS send over the CAN bus to the main ECU?')
sendPrompt('What types of switches are used for active cell balancing — MOSFETs, flying capacitors, or inductors?')
sendPrompt('Explain P-MGMT power management in a BMS ASIC — how does it handle sleep mode and why does it listen to the BUSY signal from A-BAL?')

svg.text: PWM — gate drive to BAL switches
Click any block to explore further · Dashed boundary = ASIC die edge
